frameworks.name: Competitive Moat Analysis
frameworks.description: Framework for assessing the durability of a company's competitive advantage
steps: 1. Identify moat type: brand, network effects, switching costs, patents, cost advantage, or scale
2. Apply the '$10 billion test' - could a well-funded competitor replicate this in 10 years?
3. Look for pricing power evidence - can they raise prices without losing customers?
4. Check returns on capital trend - stable or growing ROIC above 15%?
5. Evaluate management's track record of capital allocation decisions

frameworks.name: Margin of Safety Calculation
frameworks.description: Framework for determining if a stock is priced with adequate downside protection
steps: 1. Calculate intrinsic value using owner earnings or DCF with 10-12% discount rate
2. Determine 'normalized' earnings - what the company can earn in average conditions
3. Apply conservative growth assumptions (GDP growth or lower for mature businesses)
4. Calculate margin of safety = (Intrinsic Value - Current Price) / Intrinsic Value
5. Require minimum 30-40% margin of safety before investing

frameworks.name: Owner Earnings Calculation
frameworks.description: True economic earnings available to owners, as defined by Buffett
frameworks.formula: Owner Earnings = Net Income + Depreciation/Amortization - Maintenance CapEx - Working Capital Changes
steps: 1. Start with reported net income
2. Add back depreciation and amortization (non-cash charges)
3. Subtract maintenance capital expenditures (estimate 50-80% of total CapEx for mature businesses)
4. Adjust for working capital changes
5. Compare to reported free cash flow and reconcile differences

frameworks.name: Circle of Competence Assessment
frameworks.description: Framework for determining if an investment is within your area of expertise
steps: 1. Can you explain the business model in simple terms?
2. Do you understand how the company makes money today?
3. Can you predict where the business will be in 10 years?
4. Do you understand the competitive dynamics?
5. If you can't answer yes to all, stay away - there are plenty of other opportunities

frameworks.name: Wonderful Company Checklist
frameworks.description: Characteristics that define a 'wonderful company' worth paying fair price for
criteria: Consistent high returns on equity (>15% over 10 years)
